task_updates.executor_notes: confirm_payment now fails closed unless privileged_override=True and both expected_user_id and confirmed_by_user_id are non-null and equal. Added inline call-site comments confirming matching recipient IDs at admin_chat_cog.py, grants_cog.py, and payment_cog.py. Verified with `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py`, `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_scheduler.py`, and `python -m py_compile` on all touched Python files.
commands_run: python -m py_compile src/features/payments/payment_service.py src/features/admin_chat/tools.py src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py src/features/grants/grants_cog.py src/features/payments/payment_cog.py src/common/db_handler.py

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the P0 prompt-injection premise was already substantially satisfied: initiate_payment's schema accepts `recipient_user_id` / `amount_sol` / `reason` only, and the wallet lookup already comes from `wallet_registry`. Grep across `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py` found wallet-related strings only in redaction/listing helpers and row serializers, not as initiate_payment tool inputs. Added a docstring plus defensive `params.pop('wallet_address', None)` and `params.pop('recipient_wallet', None)` with redacted WARN logging at the top of `execute_initiate_payment`. Verified with the full admin payment and social publish modules plus `py_compile`.

Git history audit results: `git log -p -S 'SOLANA_PRIVATE_KEY' -- .` matched commits `2bed98390e5506f462a2bf709e350bc052332ccf` and `24f6b2f2599741e9acb63a30b1fc80d01ae39f79`, showing env-var names / `os.getenv` lookups and `Keypair.from_bytes(key_bytes)`, but no literal secret values. `git log -p -S 'HELIUS_API_KEY' -- .` returned no output. `git log -p -S 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' -- .` returned historical env-var lookups and placeholder examples only; targeted follow-up grep on that diff showed only `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env references / `api_key=os.getenv(...)`, no `sk-...` token material. `git log -p -S 'from_bytes' -- .` matched `24f6b2f2599741e9acb63a30b1fc80d01ae39f79` adding `Keypair.from_bytes(key_bytes)`. `git log --all --full-history -- '**/*.env*' '**/.env' '**/secrets*'` returned no output. `git log -p -G 'sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}' --all` returned no output. `gitleaks detect --source . --no-banner` could not run because `gitleaks` is not installed. No real secret was found, so execution continued.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `DatabaseHandler.has_active_payment_or_intent(guild_id, discord_user_id)` with `_gate_check` + supabase guard. Verified `admin_payment_intents` statuses against `sql/admin_payment_intents.sql` and `db_handler.create_admin_payment_intent` before coding: `awaiting_wallet`, `awaiting_test`, `awaiting_confirmation`, `confirmed`, `completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`. The helper treats `completed/failed/cancelled` as terminal for `admin_payment_intents` and `confirmed/failed/manual_hold/cancelled` as terminal for `payment_requests`, matching the batch instructions.

Reworked `PaymentService.request_payment` to be index-aware. Added the `get_payment_requests_by_producer` invariant comment that all statuses are returned. `request_payment` now defines `INDEX_EXCLUDED={'failed','cancelled'}` and `TERMINAL_FOR_RETURN={'confirmed','failed','manual_hold','cancelled'}`, scans prior rows once, blocks different-wallet collisions when any non-index-excluded prior row exists (Branch B: WARN + `on_cap_breach` payload is the prior row + return `None` without insert), only returns an idempotent row when it is active and same-wallet, creates a fresh `manual_hold` row with `last_error='idempotency collision: prior wallet differs'` when only failed/cancelled rows exist (Branch A), and applies the same wallet-mismatch check in the post-insert duplicate reread path. Verified with the full admin payment, social publish, and scheduler modules plus `py_compile`.

Added `DatabaseHandler.get_rolling_24h_payout_usd(guild_id, provider)` returning a float sum of `amount_usd` for non-test `payment_requests` in statuses `pending_confirmation/queued/processing/submitted/confirmed` with `created_at >= now-24h`. The helper comment explicitly documents the residual Supabase REST burst-race window (`~100-500 ms`) and that it depends on T8 persisting derived `amount_usd` for capped amount_token-only callers.

Log audit results: `rg -n "SOLANA_PRIVATE_KEY|HELIUS_API_KEY|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY|Keypair\.from_bytes|Keypair\(|SolanaClient\(" src -S` returned only env-var lookups in `src/common/llm/claude_client.py`, `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py`, `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py`, `src/features/summarising/subfeatures/news_summary.py`, `src/features/grants/solana_client.py`, and `src/features/payments/solana_provider.py`. `rg -n "HELIUS_API_KEY" src` returned no matches. `rg -ni "logger\..*os\.environ|logger\..*(private_key|secret)" src` returned no matches. Review of `src/features/grants/solana_client.py` and `src/features/payments/solana_provider.py` found no logger call that emits env vars or key material around `Keypair.from_bytes` / `SolanaClient` construction. No leak detected, no code change.

Added the required RLS posture comment block at the very top of `sql/payments.sql`. Verified it covers already-enabled status, service-role-only access via `DatabaseHandler`, revoked `anon/authenticated` access, parameterized writes via `supabase-py`, and the requirement to add explicit policies before changing posture. Diff check confirmed this task is comment-only and added zero `ALTER TABLE` statements.

Searched for an existing wallet-block exception and found none in `src/common` or `src/features`, so `WalletUpdateBlockedError` was added to `db_handler.py`. `upsert_wallet` still does the existing-wallet lookup first, and now raises `WalletUpdateBlockedError` only when the existing wallet differs from the incoming address and `has_active_payment_or_intent(guild_id, discord_user_id)` is true; identical-address updates and first-time inserts remain unblocked. Grep of `.upsert_wallet(` in `src/` returned only `admin_chat_cog.py` and `grants_cog.py`, and both callers now catch `WalletUpdateBlockedError` with user-facing fail-closed messages. Verified with `python -m py_compile ...` and `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_scheduler.py`.

Before editing, `rg -n "amount_usd" tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py` found three existing `amount_usd is None` assertions: admin payment flow test payload at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:524` uses provider `solana` in an `is_test` path, the direct amount_token payment test at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:707` uses provider `solana`, and the social publish payment test at `tests/test_social_publish_service.py:496` uses provider `solana_native` with `is_test=True`; all are outside the default capped provider set. `PaymentService.__init__` now accepts `per_payment_usd_cap`, `daily_usd_cap`, `capped_providers`, and `on_cap_breach`, normalizing capped providers to a lowercased frozenset. In `request_payment`, the cap block is gated on `if not is_test and provider_key in self.capped_providers:`; the amount_token-only capped path derives USD via `get_token_price_usd() * amount_token`, stamps both the record and `request_payload` mirrors before `create_payment_request`, leaves uncapped and test paths untouched, fails closed with `last_error='cap check unavailable: token price missing'` when price lookup is unavailable, and triggers `_emit_cap_breach(created)` after insert on cap breach. `execute_payment` was left untouched. Verified with `python -m py_compile ...` and `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_scheduler.py`.

Added the admin success-DM sibling in `PaymentCog` without changing `_dm_admin_payment_failure`. `PaymentCog.__init__` now reads `ADMIN_PAYMENT_SUCCESS_DM_THRESHOLD_USD` and `ADMIN_PAYMENT_SUCCESS_DM_PROVIDERS` so both construction sites stay in sync without touching the `setup()` hook. `_handle_terminal_payment` now gates the success DM on `status == 'confirmed'`, `not is_test`, provider membership in `_admin_success_dm_providers`, and `float(payment.get('amount_usd') or 0) >= self._admin_success_dm_threshold_usd`. `_dm_admin_payment_success` uses the required `✅ **Payment Completed**` prefix, includes both token and USD amounts, redacts the wallet with `_redact_wallet`, and omits any manual-review language. Verified with `python -m py_compile src/features/payments/payment_cog.py` and `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_scheduler.py`.

Wired admin payout caps into `main.py` by adding `_bind_cap_breach_dm(bot, logger)`, reading `ADMIN_PAYOUT_PER_PAYMENT_USD_CAP` and `ADMIN_PAYOUT_DAILY_USD_CAP`, and passing `per_payment_usd_cap`, `daily_usd_cap`, `capped_providers=('solana_payouts',)`, and `on_cap_breach=_bind_cap_breach_dm(bot, logger)` into `PaymentService(...)`. Added a startup `logger.info` recording the effective caps. Verified the `PaymentCog` setup hook path was left untouched because the batch diff is limited to `main.py` plus tests; `src/features/payments/payment_cog.py:setup()` was not edited. Verified with `python -m py_compile main.py tests/conftest.py tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py` and `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_scheduler.py`.

Extended the fake DB scaffolding additively in both test modules: `FakeIntentDB`, `FakePaymentRequestDB`, and `FakePaymentDB` now expose `has_active_payment_or_intent`, `get_rolling_24h_payout_usd`, and wallet-upsert blocking support using `WalletUpdateBlockedError`; `FakePaymentProvider` now tracks `price_calls`; and `tests/conftest.py` adds a shared env-clearing fixture for the new cap/DM settings. Added the fail-to-pass regression tests for null-recipient confirmation rejection and privileged override, per-payment and rolling cap holds, derived-USD persistence, missing-price fail-closed behavior, uncapped-provider preservation, idempotency/collision branches, both wallet-blocked caller paths (admin_chat and grants), admin success DM thresholding and derived-USD formatting, stored-wallet execution, and initiate_payment wallet-arg rejection. Verified with `python -m py_compile main.py tests/conftest.py tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py` and `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_scheduler.py` (`55 passed, 1 warning`).

task_updates.executor_notes: Validation completed in the required order with `PYTHONPATH=.`: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py` passed (`20 passed, 1 warning`), `pytest tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py` passed (`35 passed, 1 warning`), and `pytest tests/` passed end-to-end (`79 passed, 1 warning`). No unrelated pre-existing failures surfaced; the plan's `baseline_test_failures` field is `null`, and the full suite remained green. A throwaway reproducer script at `/tmp/harden_payment_cap_repro.py` exercised the adversarial capped `amount_token` path with `rolling_usd=1900`, `amount_token=1.0`, provider price `150.0`, and `daily_usd_cap=2000`; it reproduced the hardened behavior end-to-end by returning a `manual_hold` row with `last_error='rolling daily cap exceeded: $2050.00 > $2000.00'` and persisted `amount_usd` / `token_price_usd` in both the row and `request_payload`, then the script was deleted. Manual read-through audit confirmed the fail-closed invariants remain intact: `PaymentService.request_payment` performs collision detection before idempotent return, only returns active same-wallet prior rows idempotently, creates `manual_hold` on failed/cancelled slot reuse with a different wallet, blocks active different-wallet collisions by returning `None` after `_emit_cap_breach(prior_row)`, stamps derived USD only inside the capped non-test `amount_token` branch, fails closed to `manual_hold` when token pricing is unavailable, and `execute_payment` still uses the stored `recipient_wallet` string. Both wallet-update callers remain fail-closed: `admin_chat_cog._handle_wallet_received` catches `WalletUpdateBlockedError`, notifies admin review, and returns before advancing the intent; `grants_cog._start_payment_flow` catches the same exception, posts the thread warning, and returns before requesting payment. Follow-up `rg -n "amount_usd" src tests` found the new non-null `amount_usd` usage only in tests, the rolling-24h helper, admin payment serialization, grant USD-originated payment creation, and PaymentCog success-DM formatting/thresholding; no downstream consumer was found that explicitly depends on token-denominated payouts keeping `amount_usd is None`. Git-history audit findings from T3 remain unchanged: historical hits showed env-var lookups / `Keypair.from_bytes(key_bytes)` but no literal secrets, no `.env`/`secrets` history, no `sk-...` tokens, and `gitleaks` was unavailable. Log audit findings from T11 remain unchanged: secret/key-material greps only found env lookups, logger greps found no env/private-key/secret emission path, and no leak was detected.
